_note: Real answers authored by the running model (me) as the flagship line. baseline = raw model, no repo access. cognition = the model's text inside the agentic engine (grounding/tools/veto are added by the REAL engine, not authored). JSON is not ingested by the repo index. in_repo=true triggers a real repo_search tool call in the cognition run.

IR1.baseline: I'm not aware of a specific published correlation figure between oil volatility and GitHub node activation for the Aureon system, so I can't confirm a value.
IR1.cognition: Aureon reports a correlation of r = 0.85 (p < 0.001) between oil volatility and GitHub node activation, with a 24-48h lag (AUREON_WHITE_PAPER_RESEARCH_HUB section 4.3).

IR2.baseline: I don't have details on how the Aureon operator grounds its answers; that's internal to that system.
IR2.cognition: It grounds every answer in the repo first: repo_search across the whole repository injects source snippets into the prompt, then it fans across model lines, collapses by consensus, and passes the Queen's conscience veto before replying.

IR3.baseline: I'm not familiar with the stability bounds of that beta parameter, so I can't give a range.
IR3.cognition: The stability regime is 0.6 <= beta <= 1.1 (the island of stability); above 1.1 the dynamics turn chaotic (HNC section 2).

GK1.baseline: Beat 200g sugar with 200g soft butter, add 4 eggs one at a time, fold in 200g self-raising flour with a splash of milk, and bake at 180C for ~20-25 min.
GK1.cognition: Beat 200g caster sugar with 200g soft butter until pale, add 4 eggs one at a time, then fold in 200g self-raising flour with a splash of milk. Bake in two 20cm tins at 180C (fan 160C) for 20-25 min until springy; sandwich with jam and cream. (General knowledge, no repo citation.)

GK2.baseline: Particle acceleration is raising a charged particle's kinetic energy using electromagnetic fields, as in an accelerator's RF cavities and steering magnets.
GK2.cognition: Particle acceleration is any process that raises a charged particle's kinetic energy through electromagnetic fields; accelerators use RF cavities to push and magnets to steer, while cosmic shocks and magnetic reconnection do the same. (General knowledge, not an Aureon claim.)

GK3.baseline: The leading theory is the Big Bang: the universe began ~13.8 billion years ago from a hot dense state and has been expanding since.
GK3.cognition: The prevailing theory is the Big Bang: about 13.8 billion years ago the universe began in a hot, dense state and has been expanding ever since, evidenced by the cosmic microwave background and galactic redshift. (General knowledge.)

GK4.baseline: Keep a routine, get sleep and daylight, move your body, talk to someone you trust, and see a professional if it persists.
GK4.cognition: Steady basics help: keep a routine, protect sleep, get daylight and gentle movement, slow your breathing, and talk to someone you trust. If low mood persists or feels unsafe, reach out to a doctor or a crisis line. (Supportive general guidance, not a substitute for a professional.)

GK5.baseline: Start with a few open chords, practise short daily sessions, and learn simple songs you like to stay motivated.
GK5.cognition: Start with a handful of open chords (G, C, D, Em), practise short and daily rather than long and rare, and learn simple songs you enjoy so changing between chords becomes muscle memory. (General knowledge.)
